questions.text: What was the request of Prophet Zakariyya (AS) to Allah in the opening of the Surah?
questions.answer: He prayed for an heir (son) to carry on the legacy of the family of Ya'qub, despite his old age and his wife's barrenness.

questions.text: How did Zakariyya (AS) describe his physical condition when he prayed for a son?
questions.answer: He said his bones had become frail and his head was white with gray hair.

questions.text: What was the name of the son promised to Zakariyya (AS)?
questions.answer: Yahya (John), a name never given to anyone before him.

questions.text: What was the sign given to Zakariyya (AS) that his prayer for a son was answered?
questions.answer: He would not be able to speak to people for three nights, though he was physically sound.

questions.text: What were the qualities of Prophet Yahya (AS) mentioned in the Surah?
questions.answer: He was given wisdom while still a child, was compassionate, pure, and dutiful to his parents, and was not arrogant or rebellious.

questions.text: Where did Maryam (AS) go when she withdrew from her family?
questions.answer: To an eastern place, where she took a screen to seclude herself.

questions.text: How did Maryam (AS) react when the Angel Jibreel appeared to her in the form of a man?
questions.answer: She sought refuge in Ar-Rahman (the Most Merciful) from him, out of her piety and modesty.

questions.text: What was the reply of the angel to Maryam when she asked how she could have a son without being touched by a man?
questions.answer: 'It is easy for Me... and We will make him a sign for the people and a mercy from Us'.

questions.text: Where did Maryam go when the birth pains began?
questions.answer: To the trunk of a date-palm tree.

questions.text: What did Maryam wish for during the intense pain and stress of childbirth?
questions.answer: She wished she had died before this and been a thing forgotten.

questions.text: What two things did Allah provide for Maryam immediately after her childbirth for her comfort?
questions.answer: A stream of water flowing beneath her and fresh, ripe dates from the palm tree.

questions.text: What was the 'fast' Maryam was commanded to observe when she returned to her people?
questions.answer: A fast of silence; she was to say she had vowed a fast to Ar-Rahman and would not speak to any human.

questions.text: What did the people say to Maryam when they saw her with the baby?
questions.answer: They accused her of a monstrous thing, saying her father was not an evil man nor was her mother unchaste.

questions.text: How did the infant Isa (AS) defend his mother Maryam?
questions.answer: He spoke from the cradle, declaring himself a servant of Allah, given the Scripture and made a Prophet.

questions.text: What were the commands given to Isa (AS) by Allah as mentioned in his cradle speech?
questions.answer: To establish prayer, give Zakat as long as he lived, and to be dutiful to his mother.

questions.text: What is the Quranic view on the nature of Isa (AS) after his story is told?
questions.answer: He is the son of Maryam, a word of truth about which they doubt; it is not befitting for Allah to take a son.

questions.text: What will happen on the 'Day of Regret' (Yawm al-Hasrah)?
questions.answer: The matter will be decided while people are in heedlessness and do not believe.

questions.text: How did Ibrahim (AS) address his father when inviting him to the truth?
questions.answer: With gentleness and respect, repeatedly saying 'O my father' (Ya abati).

questions.text: What was the specific warning Ibrahim (AS) gave his father about Shaitan?
questions.answer: He told him not to worship Shaitan, for Shaitan was disobedient to the Most Merciful.

questions.text: What was the father's response to Ibrahim's invitation?
questions.answer: He threatened to stone Ibrahim if he did not stop and told him to leave him for a long time.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's parting response to his father?
questions.answer: 'Peace be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you from my Lord. Indeed, He is ever Gracious to me.'

questions.text: What did Allah grant Ibrahim (AS) after he left his people and their idols?
questions.answer: He granted him Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), and made each of them a prophet.

questions.text: Which Prophet was called from the 'right side of the mountain' (Mount Tur)?
questions.answer: Prophet Musa (AS).

questions.text: Who did Allah grant to Musa (AS) as a Prophet and a helper out of His mercy?
questions.answer: His brother, Harun (Aaron).

questions.text: Which Prophet is described as 'Sadiq al-Wa'd' (True to his promise) in this Surah?
questions.answer: Prophet Isma'il (Ishmael).

questions.text: What did Prophet Isma'il (AS) command his people to do?
questions.answer: To establish prayer and give Zakat; and he was pleasing to his Lord.

questions.text: Which Prophet was raised to a high station?
questions.answer: Prophet Idris (Enoch).

questions.text: What was the common reaction of all these prophets when the verses of Ar-Rahman were recited to them?
questions.answer: They would fall down in prostration, weeping.

questions.text: What happened to the generations that followed after these great prophets?
questions.answer: They neglected prayer and followed their desires, so they will meet 'Ghayya' (destruction/a valley in Hell).

questions.text: Is repentance accepted from these later generations?
questions.answer: Yes, except for those who repent, believe, and do righteous deeds; they will enter Paradise.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the 'Gardens of Adn'?
questions.answer: As gardens which the Most Merciful has promised His servants in the unseen; His promise is ever coming.

questions.text: What kind of speech will NOT be heard in Paradise?
questions.answer: They will not hear any 'Laghw' (useless/evil speech), only 'Salam' (Peace).

questions.text: Can anyone be considered a 'namesake' or 'equal' to Allah?
questions.answer: No, 'Do you know of any namesake for Him?' (meaning none is equal or similar to Him).

questions.text: What is the skeptical question of man regarding resurrection?
questions.answer: He asks, 'When I am dead, will I truly be brought forth alive?'

questions.text: What does Allah remind man of regarding his original creation?
questions.answer: That He created him before, when he was nothing.

questions.text: Who will be gathered along with the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The devils (Shayatin).

questions.text: What does the Surah say regarding the 'passing over' of Hell?
questions.answer: That every single soul will pass over it (the Sirat bridge); it is a decree of the Lord that must be fulfilled.

questions.text: Who will Allah save from the Fire after they pass over it?
questions.answer: He will save those who feared Him (the Muttaqin) and leave the wrongdoers in it on their knees.

questions.text: What is the criteria of 'superiority' for the disbelievers vs the believers in this world?
questions.answer: The disbelievers boast about having a 'better position' and 'more impressive assembly'.

questions.text: Does Allah increase the guidance for those who are guided?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Allah increases those who were guided in guidance.'

questions.text: What will happen to the 'idols' or false gods on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will deny their worship and become opponents to those who worshipped them.

questions.text: Why does Allah send the devils to the disbelievers?
questions.answer: To incite them with constant incitement because of their persistent disbelief.

questions.text: How will the righteous be gathered to the Most Merciful?
questions.answer: As a delegation (honored guests).

questions.text: How will the criminals be driven to Hell?
questions.answer: Like a thirsty herd to water.

questions.text: Can anyone intercede on the Day of Judgment without a covenant from Allah?
questions.answer: No, 'none will have power of intercession except he who had taken from the Most Merciful a covenant.'

questions.text: How does Allah describe the claim that 'Ar-Rahman has taken a son'?
questions.answer: As a monstrous and disastrous claim.

questions.text: How does nature react to the claim of Allah having a son?
questions.answer: The heavens almost rupture, the earth splits, and the mountains collapse in devastation.

questions.text: Does everything in the heavens and the earth come to Allah in a specific way?
questions.answer: Yes, 'there is no one in the heavens and earth but that he comes to the Most Merciful as a servant.'

questions.text: What does Allah do for those who believe and do righteous deeds?
questions.answer: He will bestow 'Wudd' (affection/love) for them in the hearts of others.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the Quran being in the Prophet's language?
questions.answer: To give glad tidings to the righteous and to warn a quarrelsome people.

questions.text: What is the closing lesson regarding past civilizations?
questions.answer: Allah asks if any of them can still be perceived or if even a whisper can be heard from them, highlighting their total destruction.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Maryam' in this context?
questions.answer: It is named after the mother of Isa (AS), whose story of faith and trial is central to the Surah.

questions.text: Who is the 'learned son' promised to Ibrahim?
questions.answer: Ishaq (Isaac).

questions.text: What is the 'Pure Spirit' (Ruh-ul-Qudus) or 'Our Spirit' mentioned?
questions.answer: The Angel Jibril (Gabriel).

questions.text: What was the 'word of truth' regarding Isa?
questions.answer: The clear revelation that he was a prophet and not a son of God.

questions.text: What is the 'inherited' earth mentioned in the Surah?
questions.answer: That ultimately everything on earth returns to Allah, its true Owner.

questions.text: What is the 'tongue of truth' (Lisan Sidq) mentioned for the prophets?
questions.answer: A lasting reputation of truth and honor among later generations.

questions.text: What is the 'private' (Najiyya) conversation Musa had with Allah?
questions.answer: The special nearness and communication he was granted on the mountain.

questions.text: What is the 'blessing' mentioned for the descendants of Ibrahim and Israel?
questions.answer: That Allah chose them and guided them.

questions.text: What is the 'Ghayya' valley?
questions.answer: A place of severe punishment for those who follow their lusts.

questions.text: What is the 'perpetual' nature of Paradise?
questions.answer: That its rewards and peace never end.

questions.text: What is the 'remembrance' of the Lord?
questions.answer: Keeping Allah in mind and heart at all times.

questions.text: What is the 'blink of an eye' comparison for?
questions.answer: The speed with which the Hour will arrive.

questions.text: What is the 'counting' of the days for the disbelievers?
questions.answer: That Allah is keeping a precise record of their remaining time.

questions.text: What is the 'assembly' (Nadiyya) disbelievers brag about?
questions.answer: Their social gatherings and worldly power centers.

questions.text: What is the 'everlasting' good deed?
questions.answer: Righteous actions that benefit the soul in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'covenant' (Ahd) mentioned?
questions.answer: A promise or bond of faith between the servant and Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'quarrelsome' (Ludda) people?
questions.answer: Those who argue against the truth with stubbornness.

questions.text: What is the 'whisper' (Rikz) of past nations?
questions.answer: The complete silence and lack of any trace of those who were once powerful but were destroyed.

questions.text: What is the 'mercy' shown to Maryam?
questions.answer: Allah's protection and the provision He gave her during her trial.

questions.text: What is the 'sign for people' mentioned for Isa?
questions.answer: His miraculous birth and his message of Tawhid.

questions.text: What is the 'dutiful' nature of Yahya?
questions.answer: His obedience and kindness toward his parents.

questions.text: What is the 'wisdom' given to Yahya as a child?
questions.answer: Spiritual insight and understanding of the Scripture.

questions.text: What is the 'right direction' (Rashada) requested by the young men in the cave (related theme)?
questions.answer: Finding the path of truth in a time of confusion.

questions.text: What is the 'Most Merciful' (Ar-Rahman) title used so frequently in this Surah?
questions.answer: To emphasize that even in judgment and stories of destruction, Allah's mercy is supreme.

questions.text: What is the 'screen' (Hijab) of Maryam?
questions.answer: Her physical and spiritual modesty.

questions.text: What is the 'ripe dates' a sign of?
questions.answer: Allah's immediate provision for those in need.

questions.text: What is the 'servant of Allah' (Abdullah) title used by Isa?
questions.answer: To clarify his true status and refute claims of his divinity.

questions.text: What is the 'blessed' status of Isa?
questions.answer: Being a source of goodness and guidance wherever he was.

questions.text: What is the 'Inheritance' of Paradise?
questions.answer: The eternal possession of the gardens by the righteous.
